_Toc158621599 \h 6 13 Questions and Answers PAGEREF _Toc158621600 \h 7 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Christopher Veau 12/ 12 /2023 0.0 Original document. EX8 8 – Ecom Order Pre-Processing D uring certain times of year, for e commerce orders , McLane has a need to include gel packs in the containers that are being shipped . This is to keep the contents from thawing/melting. McLane currently has a gel pack logic called the BOLDTSMITH logic which is being used to determine ho w many gel packs are needed in a container based off the containers contents as well as the weather forecast. In WM cubing occurs during waving, with base there i s not a way for WM to send BOLDTSMITH the oLPN contents before cubing occurs so that when cubing occurs the number of gel packs can be included in the logic. To resolve this problem, McLane ’s will indicate to WM the volume of gel packs needed for the induvial item and their quantities throughout the order. WM is enhanced with this extension to run a custom pre-processor. The pre-processor updates the unit volume field on the order line to include the gel pack volume . When WM carries out its cubing it will allow for enough room in the containers for the gel packs to be added after picking. This modification allows for a decent estimation of the additional volume needed for gel packs in the cubing logic in order to mitigate needing to repack contents in different containers after the actual number of gel pack s for a container and its contents is determined after picking . Note: the logic to attain the actual number of gel pack is defined in EX89. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y Order Pre-Processing Before an order reaches WM, McLane’s Hu b is to make a call to the ir Ecom API which has the BOLDTSMITH logic . The BOLDTSMITH logic will return the volume of gel packs needed for each order line and McLane’s Hu b will appropriately populate the ORDER_LINE_ITEM . INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_NBR field on the order before sending to Manhattan . The o rder ’ s s tatus is also updated by McLane’s Hub to be in “Draft” status. Once an order is bridged into WM in Draft status WM calculates and updates the unit volume for the order lines . Once the order lines are appropriately updated WM updates the order status to “R elease d” . Assumptions All Ecom orders have order type as “ECOM” McLane Hub connect s to Ecom API which runs the Boldtsmith logic and send s the order line level gel pack volume needed for that order line All Ecom orders will be bridged into WM in “Draft” status The order lines that have INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_NBR as Null or 0 are assumed to not need gel packs The INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_NBR is populated in units of cubic inches Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement DistributionOrder Message>DistributionOrder> LineItem> InternalOrderSequenceNbr Value populated by McLane Hub based off BOLDTSMITH logic Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Not required for this extension UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension Process Change/Addition Distribution Order Pre-Processor Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX8 8 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX8 8 is turned off, and no custom EX8 8 processing will occur. Validation /Selection When the Distribution Order Pre-Processor is executed, it selects all Ecom D Os ( ORDERS.ORDER_TYPE = ‘ECOM’) and that are in “Draft” Status (ORDERS.DO_STATUS = 100) AND have no import errors (ORDE R S.HAS_IMPORT_ERROR = 0). Updates Once WM finds the valid orders to update , the following logic is carried out . For each order line on the order WM takes the current unit volume (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.UNIT_VOL) for the item and multiplies it by the order lines quantity (ORDER_LINE_ITEM. ORDER_QTY) to get the T otal I tem V olume. WM then takes the T otal I tem V olume and adds the gel pack volume provided by McLane’s ecommerce system which is populated on ORDER_LINE_ITEM.INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_NBR to get the T otal L ine V olume . This T otal L ine V olume is then divided by the item quantity (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_QTY) and WM replaces the unit volume (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.UNIT_VOL) by this new unit volume for that order line. Example: Unit volume = 2 00 cu inches Item Quantity = 5 Order line g el pack volume = 43 cubic inches (5* 200 ) +43 = 1043 1043/5 = 208.6 208.6 becomes the new unit volume Navigation No changes to base Navigation as part of this extension. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label, or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 8 8 EX 8 8 Extension Control EX 8 8 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX 8 8 Extension Control is turned ON Database Not required for this extension.
